{{# @name Form handler (project override) @desc Override of Peak Tools' upstream `form_handler` snippet that survives Livewire's `wire:navigate` transitions. The upstream version registers `formHandler` inside an event listener for Alpine's `initializing` lifecycle event. That event only fires once per session — on the initial page load. When a visitor reaches a form page via `wire:navigate` from a page without the form (any nav click, the tour CTA into `/booking`, etc.), the inline script attaches a listener for an event that has already fired and `formHandler` is never registered. Alpine then evaluates `x-data="formHandler()"` against `undefined`, the subtree fails to hydrate, and the visitor sees only the static submit button. This override registers immediately when Alpine is already live (every `wire:navigate` after the first page), and otherwise defers to `alpine:init` (the very first load). It also re-runs `Alpine.initTree` on any already-mounted form wrappers whose data stack is missing the `submit` method — covering the race where the morph initialises the new x-data before the inline script registers `formHandler`. #}} {{ once }} {{# The Statamic helpers for conditional fields. #}} {{ /once }}
